title: Jurassic Bark

jokes.text: And nothing in my head.
jokes.setup: Magic trick setup about having nothing up sleeves
jokes.punchline: Self-deprecating admission of stupidity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender line - self-aware stupidity delivered with confidence. Great character integration and economy.

jokes.text: Amazing! That's why they call you Bender the Magnificent. / No, it isn't.
jokes.punchline: Bender's blunt correction
jokes.explanation: Simple deflation joke. Bender's honesty undercuts the moment nicely.

jokes.text: Interesting. No, wait, the other thing. Tedious.
jokes.punchline: Deliberate correction to opposite sentiment
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection structure. The fake-out format is classic and perfectly delivered. High quotability.

jokes.text: You lived before you met me? / Sure. Lots of people did.
jokes.setup: Fry's innocent question about his past life
jokes.punchline: Bender's matter-of-fact response treating it as profound
jokes.explanation: Bender treating obvious fact as revelation. Good character moment but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Truly, they were as gods who built this place.
jokes.punchline: Applied to mundane 20th century pizzeria
jokes.explanation: Classic juxtaposition of reverent language with mundane subject. Solid but predictable.

jokes.text: Scientists theorize it was used to gently discipline the delivery boy.
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic description of workplace abuse
jokes.explanation: Good euphemistic humor. 'Gently discipline' for paddling is a nice touch.

jokes.text: It was also used to move pizzas and crush rats.
jokes.punchline: Casual addition of rat crushing
jokes.explanation: Multi-purpose tool joke with escalation to violence. Solid character moment for Fry.

jokes.text: I am a volunteer housewife with 45 minutes of orientation and a Harlequin romance about archeologists.
jokes.punchline: Completely inadequate qualifications
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of inadequate credentials. The Harlequin romance detail is perfect specificity.

jokes.text: Don't wave your fancy degrees at me.
jokes.setup: Museum guide just admitted having no qualifications
jokes.punchline: Fry treating them as impressive credentials
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry stupidity - treating obvious lack of credentials as intimidating expertise.

jokes.text: Pizza delivery for Mr. Seymour Asses?
jokes.punchline: Prank name that sounds like 'see more asses'
jokes.explanation: Classic prank call name. Low originality but functional and memorable within the story.

jokes.text: I hope that in time you'll realize what an idiot you've been. / I wouldn't count on that.
jokes.setup: Customer's condescending hope for Fry's growth
jokes.punchline: Fry's honest self-assessment
jokes.explanation: Good character moment. Fry's oblivious honesty about his own limitations.

jokes.text: Here. But if Mr. Panucci asks, your name is Seymour Asses.
jokes.punchline: Using the prank name for the dog
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that also shows Fry's kindness. Naming the dog after the prank is endearing.

jokes.text: You're not constantly judging me, like all the other dogs.
jokes.setup: Fry explaining why he likes Seymour
jokes.punchline: Paranoia about judgmental dogs
jokes.explanation: Absurd but relatable insecurity projected onto dogs. Good character insight.

jokes.text: You call no man 'Mister.'
jokes.punchline: Attributing rebellious anti-authority stance to a dog
jokes.explanation: Absurd projection of dignity onto a stray dog. Good economy and character voice.

jokes.text: Well, goodbye. Live long and prosper.
jokes.punchline: Uses Star Trek farewell to a dog
jokes.explanation: Simple pop culture reference. Shows Fry's nerd tendencies but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: That's the least appetizing calzone I've ever seen.
jokes.setup: Looking at what appears to be food
jokes.punchline: It's actually a fossilized dog
jokes.explanation: Visual gag setup. Good misdirection about what we're looking at.

jokes.text: Ow! Ow! I'm taking him home and I'd like to see anyone try and stop me. Ow! Oh! Ow! Oh! Ah!
jokes.setup: Fry's defiant challenge to stop him
jokes.punchline: Immediate and ongoing physical punishment
jokes.explanation: Standard slapstick. The defiance immediately followed by pain is classic but not innovative.

jokes.text: That's like digging up Lassie and putting her in the Louvre. / Lassie is on display in the Louvre. / I know. I was deliberately describing a similar situation.
jokes.punchline: It's actually literally true in the future
jokes.explanation: Great worldbuilding joke. What seems like bad analogy is actually perfect. Fry's defensive clarification is funny.

jokes.text: Always whining that people don't treat their ancestors' bones with respect.
jokes.punchline: Dismissive attitude toward legitimate grievances
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about colonization. Works as character moment for Leela's casual insensitivity.

jokes.text: Protesting never works. / You're right. I'll give it a shot!
jokes.punchline: Immediately decides to try it anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry logic. His contradictory thinking patterns are well-established.

jokes.text: What do you want? Fry's dog! When do you want it? Fry's dog!
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical repetition of same phrase for both questions
jokes.explanation: Subverts protest chant format. Simple but effective absurdist humor.

jokes.text: It says here that this part of The Hustle implores the gods to grant a favor, usually a Trans Am.
jokes.punchline: Sacred ritual for mundane 1970s car
jokes.explanation: Excellent juxtaposition of sacred and mundane. Trans Am is perfectly specific and absurd.

jokes.text: Just like that huge mushroom in my shower.
jokes.punchline: Comparison to disgusting bathroom fungus
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry observation - ruining a touching moment with gross personal detail.

jokes.text: You been swimming in the sewer again? You rascal.
jokes.punchline: Treating disgusting behavior as charming mischief
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment. The affection despite gross behavior is endearing.

jokes.text: There's dog fur on my slice! / No, that's vermicelli. No fur in here.
jokes.setup: Customer complaint about hair in food
jokes.punchline: Obvious lie about pasta resemblance
jokes.explanation: Standard restaurant hygiene joke. Panucci's obvious lie is mildly amusing.

jokes.text: He can do two things at one time: eat and swim. / Three things!
jokes.punchline: Implication of urinating while eating and swimming
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor. Economy is good but relies on cheap bodily function joke.

jokes.text: You'll come down with a fever of some sort.
jokes.punchline: Vague, unhelpful medical diagnosis
jokes.explanation: Mild scientist characterization joke. The vague medical terminology is mildly amusing.

jokes.text: Or as some call it, the Beelersaurus.
jokes.punchline: Self-aggrandizing nickname creation
jokes.explanation: Good character vanity moment. Scientists naming things after themselves is relatable.

jokes.text: He was once intimate with the leg of a wandering saxophonist.
jokes.punchline: Euphemistic description of dog humping behavior
jokes.explanation: Good euphemistic humor. 'Intimate with the leg' is a nice way to describe humping.

jokes.text: The 29? Interesting.
jokes.punchline: Scientist finds this mundane detail scientifically significant
jokes.explanation: Scientist overreacting to trivial detail. Good character beat but minor impact.

jokes.text: I was all in this part! It's magic! / You're not fit to wear Fry's leotard!
jokes.punchline: Bender's oddly specific insult
jokes.explanation: Great specific insult. 'Not fit to wear Fry's leotard' is wonderfully absurd and character-specific.

jokes.text: And he's looking for a garbage can to put the rock in. Here you go, buddy.
jokes.punchline: Professor assumes Fry wants to throw away the fossil
jokes.explanation: Good Professor moment. His oblivious helpfulness undermines the dramatic moment.

jokes.text: A little land mammal.
jokes.punchline: Bender's dismissive scientific terminology
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender dismissiveness. Reduces emotional moment to clinical terminology.

jokes.text: Look! There's a smidge of toe still in here.
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of leftover body part from previous cloning
jokes.explanation: Gross scientific detail delivered casually. Good worldbuilding humor about the Professor's standards.

jokes.text: There's still a creamy core of dog nougat.
jokes.punchline: Describing organic matter as candy filling
jokes.explanation: Excellent metaphor. 'Dog nougat' is wonderfully absurd and memorable.

jokes.text: So will Seymour remember how to sing 'Walking on Sunshine'?
jokes.punchline: Fry's priorities focus on silly trick rather than emotional connection
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry character moment. Missing the emotional significance for the novelty trick.

jokes.text: In your face, Grim Reaper! Crappy, ineffective reaper.
jokes.punchline: Escalating to personal insults against Death itself
jokes.explanation: Good Fry moment. His juvenile taunting of abstract concepts is in character.

jokes.text: The dog says: 'Arf.' Oh-Oh. This may take a while.
jokes.setup: Machine attempting to translate from human mode
jokes.punchline: Computer struggling with basic dog sound
jokes.explanation: Good tech humor. The machine's struggle with basic translation is relatable.

jokes.text: Like you got squat to celebrate. You're a delivery boy this millennium and you'll be a delivery boy next millennium.
jokes.punchline: Brutally accurate prediction about his lack of advancement
jokes.explanation: Dark but accurate assessment. The millennium timeframe adds weight to the insult.

jokes.text: Or he thinks your pants is too short. Which is crazy, because frankly, you look fabulous. Now get going!
jokes.punchline: Unexpected compliment followed by dismissal
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment. Panucci's unexpected kindness before sending Fry away.

jokes.text: I.C. Wiener? Oh, crud.
jokes.punchline: Realizing it's another prank name (icy wiener)
jokes.explanation: Another prank name in the series' pilot episode setup. Low craft but plot-necessary.

jokes.text: A subscription to the Daily Growl? That's not a reputable journal of opinion.
jokes.punchline: Treating dog magazine with journalistic standards
jokes.explanation: Good Professor intellectualism. Applying academic standards to a dog magazine.

jokes.text: Why waste time on a creature of inferior intelligence? / Hey, he was smart. He could fetch. / I can fetch!
jokes.punchline: Desperately competing with basic dog trick
jokes.explanation: Excellent character work. Bender's jealousy making him compete with a dog on the dog's level.

jokes.text: He could dig up bones. / Hello? Charlemagne?
jokes.punchline: Bender one-upping with historical figure's remains
jokes.explanation: Great escalation. Bender's casual access to historical remains is perfect one-upmanship.

jokes.text: No one ever asked if Bender would like to live in a tiny little house.
jokes.punchline: Jealous self-pity over doghouse
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender jealousy. His wounded feelings over a doghouse are both pathetic and endearing.

jokes.text: A tiny little house that says 'Bender' on it.
jokes.punchline: Specific detail about name on the house
jokes.explanation: Great character specificity. Bender wanting his name on the house shows his ego even in vulnerability.

jokes.text: They're still available for $45 each.
jokes.punchline: Selling used towels at ridiculous price
jokes.explanation: Good character detail. Fry's entrepreneurial delusions about worthless items.

jokes.text: Hello, first bologna of the new millennium.
jokes.punchline: Treating lunch meat as historically significant
jokes.explanation: Nice Y2K moment. The ceremonial treatment of mundane food is endearing.

jokes.text: I'm telling you, the Y2K computers got him. We'll face burning roads, rivers exploding, calculators transformed into Scud missiles.
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd technological disasters
jokes.explanation: Good period-specific humor. Y2K paranoia escalating to absurd scenarios.

jokes.text: It's the Y2K trying to lure us into an ambush. And I ain't buying it. Pass the bologna.
jokes.setup: Dog trying to lead them somewhere
jokes.punchline: Paranoid interpretation followed by casual meal request
jokes.explanation: Great paranoia humor. The casual bologna request undercuts the dramatic suspicion.

jokes.text: In case an enemy knocks the laser guns out of our hands and they slide way across the room.
jokes.setup: Explaining need for hand-to-hand combat training
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and unlikely scenario
jokes.explanation: Good specificity humor. The elaborate scenario for why they need training is amusing.

jokes.text: Of course, after he goes, you have to refill the canisters.
jokes.punchline: Mechanical waste disposal system
jokes.explanation: Functional robot pet humor. The mechanical waste system is mildly clever.

jokes.text: Robo-Puppy preparing to lick cheek. Robo-Puppy commencing cheek licking. Licking in progress. Licking complete.
jokes.punchline: Over-detailed narration of simple action
jokes.explanation: Good robot humor. The excessive narration contrasts with natural pet behavior.

jokes.text: Robo-Puppy mistreatment alert! Robo-Puppy mistreatment alert!
jokes.punchline: Automated distress signal
jokes.explanation: Good payoff for Bender's jealousy. The automated alert system is logical and funny.

jokes.text: I speak, of course, of molten lava deep within the Earth's core.
jokes.setup: Building up to power source revelation
jokes.punchline: Absurdly dangerous power source for simple cloning
jokes.explanation: Good Professor over-engineering. Using Earth's core for cloning is appropriately excessive.

jokes.text: Know ye now what feels like to be dog god!
jokes.punchline: Megalomaniacal declaration of godhood
jokes.explanation: Good Fry power-trip moment. His grandiose language about dog cloning fits his character.

jokes.text: But if we don't perform, in what sense do we have an act?
jokes.setup: Trying to convince Fry to do talent show
jokes.punchline: Philosophical question about the nature of performance
jokes.explanation: Good pseudo-philosophical Bender moment. The existential question is unexpectedly thoughtful.

jokes.text: But if you love your dog so much, maybe you'd rather play fetch!
jokes.punchline: Throws fossil like fetch toy
jokes.explanation: Perfect character climax for Bender's jealousy arc. The destructive 'fetch' with the fossil is emotionally devastating and perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Indeed. You see, that fossil was made of dolomite, the tough, black mineral that won't cop out when there's heat all about.
jokes.punchline: Rhyming geological description
jokes.explanation: Excellent rhyming scientific explanation. The internal rhyme makes it memorable and fun.

jokes.text: It's dolomite, baby!
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastic catchphrase about the mineral
jokes.explanation: Great callback with enthusiasm. The Professor's sudden hip delivery is unexpectedly charming.

jokes.text: Because I love you. Not in the way of the ancient Greeks, but the way a robot loves a human, a human loves a dog, and occasionally, a gorilla loves a kitty.
jokes.setup: Bender explaining his love for Fry
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific and absurd love analogies
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment. The escalating analogies are both touching and absurd, perfect for Bender.

jokes.text: I'm 40 percent dolomite!
jokes.setup: Professor explaining only dolomite can survive lava
jokes.punchline: Bender's convenient composition revelation
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback and character moment. Bender's percentage-based composition claims are a running gag.

jokes.text: Oh, it's hot. Oh, it's very hot!
jokes.setup: Bender enters lava to save fossil
jokes.punchline: States obvious fact about lava temperature
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy. Bender's obvious observation about lava temperature is predictable but fits the heroic moment structure.

jokes.text: You can't solve all your problems by freezing them. / I think you're forgetting our motto.
jokes.punchline: Cryogenic company's literal business model
jokes.explanation: Good irony. Fry criticizing exactly what the cryogenic company does professionally.

jokes.text: Sorry we're late. We all got sick from eating bad bologna. / Y2K.
jokes.punchline: Father blaming Y2K for food poisoning
jokes.explanation: Good callback to father's Y2K obsession. His instant blame attribution is in character.

jokes.text: Hey, where'd everybody go?
jokes.punchline: Everyone has left during his absence
jokes.explanation: Good anticlimax. Bender's heroic return undermined by everyone leaving.

jokes.text: Let this abomination unto the Lord begin!
jokes.punchline: Religious condemnation of his own scientific work
jokes.explanation: Great Professor moment. Self-aware blasphemy while proceeding anyway is perfect character work.

jokes.text: Oh, sure. Smash the smart guy's machine.
jokes.punchline: Professor's wounded intellectual pride
jokes.explanation: Good Professor reaction. His ego being bruised by the machine destruction is in character.

jokes.text: 'Walking on Sunshine' sucks noodles.
jokes.punchline: Blunt dismissal of cheerful pop song
jokes.explanation: Good Leela bluntness. 'Sucks noodles' is an amusing intensifier.

tmdb_overview: Bender grows jealous when Fry attempts to clone Seymour, his beloved fossilized dog.
